title: PHYLLOQUINONE
description: Phylloquinone (Vitamin K1) is a fat-soluble vitamin essential for blood clotting and bone metabolism, functioning as a coenzyme for vitamin K-dependent carboxylase. Found primarily in green leafy vegetables, it plays critical roles in synthesizing clotting factors like prothrombin, regulating vascular calcification through proteins like matrix Gla-protein (MGP), and may support brain health through sphingolipid biosynthesis and ferroptosis inhibition.

research.title: Vitamin K Properties in Stroke and Alzheimer's Disease: A Narrative Review
research.summary: Vitamin K deficiency increases risk of hemorrhages, atherosclerosis, and neurodegeneration, while adequate levels may benefit both vascular and cerebral health through sphingolipid biosynthesis and ferroptosis inhibition pathways.

research.title: Vitamin K - Sources, Physiological Role, Kinetics, Deficiency, Detection, Therapeutic Use, and Toxicity
research.summary: Comprehensive review of vitamin K's role in coagulation, bone metabolism, tissue calcification, and antioxidant activity, with phylloquinone being the main dietary form concentrated in green leafy vegetables.

sentiment.summary: Users show moderate awareness of vitamin K's importance, particularly when combined with vitamin D, though discussions focus more on dosing concerns and multivitamin formulations rather than standalone benefits. Most view it as a necessary but unremarkable component of supplement regimens.

effects.label: Primary Function:
effects.description: Highly effective for blood clotting function with well-established physiological role

effects.label: Cardiovascular Benefits:
effects.description: Shows promise for reducing atherosclerosis and vascular calcification in research

effects.label: Brain Health:
effects.description: Emerging evidence suggests dual benefits for vascular and cerebral health

effects.label: Synergistic Effects:
effects.description: Often combined with vitamin D for enhanced bone and cardiovascular benefits

dosage.label: Minimum Effective Dose:
dosage.description: 50 mcg of phylloquinone (K1) satisfies recommended daily intake

dosage.label: Maximum Safe Dose:
dosage.description: Up to 1,000 mcg vitamin K1 considered safe for most individuals

dosage.label: Combination Products:
dosage.description: Often formulated with 200-1,500 mcg K1 alongside vitamin D and K2 forms

dosage.label: Dietary Sources:
dosage.description: Green leafy vegetables provide primary dietary phylloquinone

sideEffects.label: Anticoagulant Interactions:
sideEffects.description: Can interfere with warfarin and similar anticoagulants requiring consistent intake monitoring

sideEffects.label: Minimal Toxicity:
sideEffects.description: Generally well-tolerated with low toxicity risk at recommended doses

sideEffects.label: No Common Complaints:
sideEffects.description: Users rarely report negative effects from vitamin K1 supplementation

sideEffects.label: Dosing Concerns:
sideEffects.description: Some users express hesitation about high-dose formulations in multivitamins

availability.label: Wide Availability:
availability.description: Found in most multivitamins and vitamin D+K combination products

availability.label: Dietary Sources:
availability.description: Abundantly available in green leafy vegetables as primary natural source

availability.label: Supplement Forms:
availability.description: Commonly available as standalone K1 or in K1+K2 complex formulations
